'''Hawk-bat Base''' was the operation center for [[Wraith Squadron]]'s [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activities while they posed as the [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force]], or Hawk-bats for short, around [[7 ABY/Legends|7.5 ABY]]. In the fight against [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], Wraith Squadron had discovered that [[Zsinj]] was recruiting pirates to his operations. The Wraiths decided to set up a false pirate operation near the planet [[Halmad/Legends|Halmad]], and chose the abandoned mining asteroid [[A3]].<ref name="Iron Fist">''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]''</ref>

Hawk-bat Base was finally abandoned when Zsinj took over Halmad, almost causing a disaster for the Wraiths in the process. Zsinj offered the "Hawk-Bats" a proposition, to work for him. Since the Hawk-bats had only five [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], the rest of the Wraiths went off to ''[[Mon Remonda]]'', the flagship of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. [[Garik Loran|Face Loran]] (General Kargin), [[Dia Passik]] (Captain Seku), [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] (Lieutenant Kettch), [[Kell Tainer]] (Lieutenant Dissek), and [[Voort saBinring|Piggy saBinring]] (Morrt) piloted the Interceptors, since [[Shalla Nelprin]]-a.k.a Qatya Nassin of the Hawk-bats, was part of the Zsinj force attacking ''[[Razor's Kiss]]''. In the subsequent battle, ''Razor's Kiss'' was destroyed, and Wraith Squadron abandoned the base.

Later, [[Gara Petothel|Lara Notsil]] came to Hawk-bat Base, after having been revealed as [[Gara Petothel]]. She left her [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] uniform there and continued on to her rendezvous point with the [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']].<ref name="Solo Command">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref>
